View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Burial Mounds |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!Type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Otokuni Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Mukō City History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!Type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otokuni Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otokuni Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Burial Mounds |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!Type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otokuni Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Burial Mounds |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!Type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Burial Mounds |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!Type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!Type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in {{Infobox ancient site |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Burial Mounds |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == (Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==

The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Burial Mounds |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Burial Mounds |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient burial mound located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Burial Mounds|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Burial Mounds in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of burial mounds in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Burial Mounds. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==

The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alias = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] (Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. (Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!Size |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=other_designation Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=other_designation Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=other_designation Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National other_designation: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designated as a National other_designation within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == Burial Facilities == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial artifacts, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National other_designation Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National other_designation designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National other_designations Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=other_designation Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Motoinari Kofun. View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |latitude_deg = 34|latitude_min = 56|latitude_sec = 42.20 |longitude_deg = 135|longitude_min = 41|longitude_sec = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments, pottery fragments |cultural_property = National other_designation: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |label = Motoinari Kofun |icon = Zenpokoho-fun-South |label_position = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been designation1 as a National other_designation within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun Period in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National other_designation Designation == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National other_designation designation of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National other_designations Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=other_designation Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]]
