revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=draft|12=draft}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 42.20 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=draft|12=draft}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|12=draft|12=draft}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Motoinari Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=draft|12=draft}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 42.20 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=draft|12=draft}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|12=draft|12=draft}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Motoinari Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Motoinari Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=draft|12=draft}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 42.20 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=draft|12=draft}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|12=draft|12=draft}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Motoinari Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Motoinari Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Motoinari Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:元稲荷古墳 up to rev 100827937
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 元稲荷古墳 |画像 = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（左に前方部、右奥に後方部） |別名 = |所属 = 向日丘陵古墳群 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[向日市]]向日町北山（勝山公園内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 56|緯度秒 = 42.20 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 50.53 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長94m |築造時期 = [[3世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者 = |出土品 = 副葬品少数・[[埴輪]]片・土器片 |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「元稲荷古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |ラベル = 元稲荷古墳 |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''元稲荷古墳'''（もといなりこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]]向日町北山にある[[古墳]]。向日丘陵古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+向日丘陵の主な古墳{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf 南孝雄 「寺戸大塚古墳 -向日丘陵の古墳時代前期の首長墓-」]}}（第242回京都市考古資料館文化財講座資料、2013年）。{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}} !代!!古墳名!!形状!!規模 |- |1||[[五塚原古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長91.2m |- |2||'''元稲荷古墳'''||[[前方後方墳]]||墳丘長94m |- |3||[[寺戸大塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長95m |- |4||[[妙見山古墳 (向日市)|妙見山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長114m |- |5||[[伝高畠陵古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径65m |}
[[京都盆地]]南西縁、[[桂川 (淀川水系)|桂川]]右岸の向日丘陵の最南端に築造された大型前方後方墳である{{Sfn|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第23号|1987}}。古墳域は[[向日神社]]境内に接し{{Sfn|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第23号|1987}}、「元稲荷」の古墳名はかつて後方部墳頂に稲荷社が鎮座したことに由来する{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}。向日丘陵では元稲荷古墳のほかにも大型前方後円墳数基が分布し、これらは「向日丘陵古墳群」と総称される{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。本古墳はこれまで、[[江戸時代]]に大規模な盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）からは計10次の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後方形で、南北方向を主軸として前方部を南方向に向ける{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は後方部が3段築成、前方部が2段築成。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められるが{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}、[[埴輪]]列はなく古い様相を示す{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、後方部中央に構築される{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この石室は盗掘に遭っているが、発掘調査では残存副葬品が検出されている。また前方部中央では埴輪片・壺形土器片の密集が認められており、[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台形埴輪]]片も検出されている{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[3世紀]]後半頃と推定される史跡説明板。。同時代の古墳のうちでは特に[[西求女塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]）と同形墳とされる。向日丘陵の首長墓群では[[五塚原古墳]]（[[前方後円墳]]）に次ぐ時期の築造に位置づけられるが、同古墳と質的な差異はないものの本古墳が前方後方形を採用する点で、五塚原古墳とは異なり[[ヤマト王権]]に対して高い自立性を持った（王権との距離の置き方が一定でなかった）様子が指摘される{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 古墳域は、[[2016年]]（[[平成]]28年）3月1日に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[梅原末治]]の紹介、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）の踏査で古墳と確認。当時は[[前方後円墳]]とされた（京都府史蹟勝地調査会）{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}。 * [[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）3月、第1次発掘調査。墳丘測量・後方部および埋葬施設の調査、前方後方墳と判明（西谷真治ら[[京都大学]]）{{Sfn|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第23号|1987}}{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）7月、第2次発掘調査。前方部の調査（[[都出比呂志]]ら京都大学）{{Sfn|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第23号|1987}}{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[2006年]]度（[[平成]]18年度）、第3次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。 * [[2007年]]度（平成19年度）、第4次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、第5次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}。 * [[2009年]]度（平成21年度）、第6次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第6次調査 現地説明会資料|2010}}。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、第7次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）。 * [[2011年]]度（平成23年度）、第8次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第8次調査 現地説明会資料|2012}}。 * [[2012年]]度（平成24年度）、第9次・第10次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第9次調査 現地説明会資料|2012}}{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）3月1日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Nishi-Motomezuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[西求女塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]）}}{{small|元稲荷古墳と同形墳とされる。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り（同形墳の[[西求女塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]）と合わせて記載）{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:90%;" !colspan=2| !!元稲荷古墳!![[西求女塚古墳]] |- !colspan=2|墳丘長 |94m||98m |- !rowspan=2|後方部!!長さ |50m||52m |- !幅 |50m||50m |- !colspan=2|くびれ部幅 |23m||25m |- !colspan=2|前方部前端幅 |47m||49m |} 墳丘の形状には、当時のヤマト王権の大王墓である[[箸墓古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]）・[[西殿塚古墳]]（奈良県[[天理市]]）との類似が指摘される{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。このことから、墳丘造営に際しては箸墓古墳がモデルプランとされ、これに西殿塚古墳の技術も援用されたと指摘される{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 Motoinari Kofun, kouhoubu-1.jpg|後方部墳頂 Motoinari Kofun, kouhoubu-2.jpg|後方部墳丘 Motoinari Kofun, kouhoubu-3.jpg|後方部（前方部から望む） Motoinari Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|前方部（後方部から望む） 元稲荷山古墳 特殊器台形埴輪・壺形埴輪.JPG|特殊器台形埴輪・壺形埴輪
{{small|[[京都大学総合博物館]]展示。}} == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Motoinari Kofun, tenjouseki.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|墳丘上の天井石残欠}}]] 埋葬施設には、後方部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が使用されている{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室の主軸は墳丘主軸と平行の南北方向で、長さは南北5.56メートル、幅は北辺で1.32メートル・南辺で1.02メートル、高さは南端で1.9メートルを測る{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。基底部は前方部墳頂と同じ高さとする{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。床面は粘土床で、長方形壇の上に粘土層（朱を塗布）・板石敷とする{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。壁面は板石積みで、上半部を持送り合掌形（または家形）とする類例のない特徴を示す{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。壁上に架けた天井石は11枚{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石室内は盗掘に遭っているため、副葬品はほとんどが散逸している{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。発掘調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 銅鏃 1 * 鉄鏃 7片 * 鉄剣 2片 * 鉄刀 3片 * 刀子 3片 * 槍 1片 * 矛石突 2片 * 錐 1片 * 鉇 数片 * 鉄斧 1 * 土師器壺破片 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 元稲荷古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3ヶ所に元稲荷古墳など8基を追加指定、「乙訓古墳群」に名称変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[京都大学総合博物館]]（京都市左京区吉田本町） - 元稲荷山古墳の出土特殊器台形埴輪を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（向日市、2014年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** 京都府発行 *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=元稲荷古墳|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/kyoutofu-J23.pdf 京都府埋蔵文化財情報]}}|issue=第23号|publisher=京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター|isbn=|pages=79-81||ref={{Harvid|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第23号|1987}}}} - リンクは京都府埋蔵文化財調査研究センター。 ** 向日市発行 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.mukoumaibun.or.jp/admin/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/P462gen.pdf 第4次発掘調査 現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}}}（向日市埋蔵文化財センター、2008年）。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.mukoumaibun.or.jp/admin/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/P469setu.pdf 第5次発掘調査 現地説明会資料「元稲荷古墳後方部後端の調査」]}}|ref={{Harvid|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}}}（向日市埋蔵文化財センター、2009年）。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.mukoumaibun.or.jp/admin/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/motoinarisetub.pdf 第6次発掘調査 現地説明会資料「元稲荷古墳後方部南西隅角の調査」]}}|ref={{Harvid|第6次調査 現地説明会資料|2010}}}}（向日市埋蔵文化財センター、2010年）。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.mukoumaibun.or.jp/admin/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/moto8GS.pdf 第8次発掘調査 現地説明会資料「元稲荷古墳前方部前半（西南側）の調査」]}}|ref={{Harvid|第8次調査 現地説明会資料|2012}}}}（向日市埋蔵文化財センター、2012年）。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.mukoumaibun.or.jp/admin/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/moto9gen_.pdf 第9次発掘調査 現地説明会資料「元稲荷古墳前方部前端、南東隅角の調査」]}}|ref={{Harvid|第9次調査 現地説明会資料|2012}}}}（向日市埋蔵文化財センター、2012年）。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.mukoumaibun.or.jp/admin/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/moto10gen.pdf 第10次発掘調査 現地説明会資料「元稲荷古墳後方部西辺・前方部南方の調査」]}}|ref={{Harvid|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}}}（向日市埋蔵文化財センター、2013年）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=元稲荷古墳|title=京都府の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]26|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490263|ref={{Harvid|元稲荷古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=元稲荷古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=元稲荷古墳|series=向日市埋蔵文化財調査報告書第101集|publisher=向日市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite 現地説明会資料|2009}}。向日丘陵では元稲荷古墳のほかにも大型前方後円墳数基が分布し、これらは「向日丘陵古墳群」と総称される{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。本古墳はこれまで、[[江戸時代]]に大規模な盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）からは計10次の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後方形で、南北方向を主軸として前方部を南方向に向ける{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は後方部が3段築成、前方部が2段築成。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められるが{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}、[[埴輪]]列はなく古い様相を示す{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、後方部中央に構築される{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この石室は盗掘に遭っているが、発掘調査では残存副葬品が検出されている。また前方部中央では埴輪片・壺形土器片の密集が認められており、[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台形埴輪]]片も検出されている{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[3世紀]]後半頃と推定される史跡説明板。。同時代の古墳のうちでは特に[[西求女塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]）と同形墳とされる。向日丘陵の首長墓群では[[五塚原古墳]]（[[前方後円墳]]）に次ぐ時期の築造に位置づけられるが、同古墳と質的な差異はないものの本古墳が前方後方形を採用する点で、五塚原古墳とは異なり[[ヤマト王権]]に対して高い自立性を持った（王権との距離の置き方が一定でなかった）様子が指摘される{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 古墳域は、[[2016年]]（[[平成]]28年）3月1日に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[梅原末治]]の紹介、[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）の踏査で古墳と確認。当時は[[前方後円墳]]とされた（京都府史蹟勝地調査会）{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}。 * [[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）3月、第1次発掘調査。墳丘測量・後方部および埋葬施設の調査、前方後方墳と判明（西谷真治ら[[京都大学]]）{{Sfn|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第23号|1987}}{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）7月、第2次発掘調査。前方部の調査（[[都出比呂志]]ら京都大学）{{Sfn|京都府埋蔵文化財情報 第23号|1987}}{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[2006年]]度（[[平成]]18年度）、第3次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。 * [[2007年]]度（平成19年度）、第4次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2008}}。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、第5次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第5次調査 現地説明会資料|2009}}。 * [[2009年]]度（平成21年度）、第6次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第6次調査 現地説明会資料|2010}}。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、第7次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）。 * [[2011年]]度（平成23年度）、第8次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第8次調査 現地説明会資料|2012}}。 * [[2012年]]度（平成24年度）、第9次・第10次発掘調査（向日市埋蔵文化財センター）{{Sfn|第9次調査 現地説明会資料|2012}}{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）3月1日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Nishi-Motomezuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[西求女塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]）}}{{small|元稲荷古墳と同形墳とされる。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り（同形墳の[[西求女塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]]）と合わせて記載）{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:90%;" !colspan=2| !!元稲荷古墳!![[西求女塚古墳]] |- !colspan=2|墳丘長 |94m||98m |- !rowspan=2|後方部!!長さ |50m||52m |- !幅 |50m||50m |- !colspan=2|くびれ部幅 |23m||25m |- !colspan=2|前方部前端幅 |47m||49m |} 墳丘の形状には、当時のヤマト王権の大王墓である[[箸墓古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]）・[[西殿塚古墳]]（奈良県[[天理市]]）との類似が指摘される{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。このことから、墳丘造営に際しては箸墓古墳がモデルプランとされ、これに西殿塚古墳の技術も援用されたと指摘される{{Sfn|第10次調査 現地説明会資料|2013}}。 Motoinari Kofun, kouhoubu-1.jpg|後方部墳頂 Motoinari Kofun, kouhoubu-2.jpg|後方部墳丘 Motoinari Kofun, kouhoubu-3.jpg|後方部（前方部から望む） Motoinari Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|前方部（後方部から望む） 元稲荷山古墳 特殊器台形埴輪・壺形埴輪.JPG|特殊器台形埴輪・壺形埴輪
{{small|[[京都大学総合博物館]]展示。}} == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Motoinari Kofun, tenjouseki.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|墳丘上の天井石残欠}}]] 埋葬施設には、後方部中央において[[竪穴式石室]]が使用されている{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室の主軸は墳丘主軸と平行の南北方向で、長さは南北5.56メートル、幅は北辺で1.32メートル・南辺で1.02メートル、高さは南端で1.9メートルを測る{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。基底部は前方部墳頂と同じ高さとする{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。床面は粘土床で、長方形壇の上に粘土層（朱を塗布）・板石敷とする{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。壁面は板石積みで、上半部を持送り合掌形（または家形）とする類例のない特徴を示す{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。壁上に架けた天井石は11枚{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 石室内は盗掘に遭っているため、副葬品はほとんどが散逸している{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。発掘調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|元稲荷古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 銅鏃 1 * 鉄鏃 7片 * 鉄剣 2片 * 鉄刀 3片 * 刀子 3片 * 槍 1片 * 矛石突 2片 * 錐 1片 * 鉇 数片 * 鉄斧 1 * 土師器壺破片 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 元稲荷古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3ヶ所に元稲荷古墳など8基を追加指定、「乙訓古墳群」に名称変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[京都大学総合博物館]]（京都市左京区吉田本町） - 元稲荷山古墳の出土特殊器台形埴輪を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（向日市、2014年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** 京都府発行 *** {{Cite (Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Motoinari Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Motoinari Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Motoinari Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=draft}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=draft}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|12=draft}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|12=draft}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|12=draft}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Motoinari Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 42.20 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}} fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|qq=draft}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|qq=draft}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 42.20 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century AD|qq=}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura River|qq=}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century AD|qq=}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Museum|qq=}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura River|qq=}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century AD|qq=}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Museum|qq=}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Moto-Inari Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Museum|qq=}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukō City History]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 42.20 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century AD|qq=}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura River|qq=}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century AD|qq=}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Museum|qq=}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = [[File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the tumulus (left: anterior section, right: posterior section) |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 42.20 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 50.53 |iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century AD|qq=}} |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-koen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}||94 m |- |3||{{ill|Terado Otsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} near the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|ja|桂川 (淀川水系)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura River|qq=}}, is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late {{ill|3rd century AD|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3rd century AD|qq=}}. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|ja|京都大学総合博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University Museum|qq=}}: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]]

(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|qq=}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|qq=}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Motoinari Kofun]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:乙訓古墳群]]
