(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Motoinari Kofun]][[en:Moto-Inari Kofun]][[ja:元稲荷古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q38279257]] Wikidata name: Motoinari Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = {{Coord|34.945056|N|135.697369|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]] |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} (Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = {{Coord|34.945056|N|135.697369|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]] |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}}

(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}}

Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = {{Coord|34.945056|N|135.697369|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]] |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279257}}

(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[co:commons:Category:Motoinari Kofun]][[en:Moto-Inari Kofun]][[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Motoinari Kofun]][[en:Moto-Inari Kofun]][[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Motoinari Kofun]][[en:Moto-Inari Kofun]][[ja:元稲荷古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q38279257]] Wikidata name: Motoinari Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Motoinari Kofun |image = File:Motoinari Kofun, zenkei.jpg |alternate_name = |group = Mukō Hills Kofuns |location = {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (inside Katsuyama Park) |coordinates = {{Coord|34.945056|N|135.697369|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|iso = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = 94 m in length |period = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]] |burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial_person = Unknown |excavated_items = Few burial goods, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments, pottery fragments |notes = National Historic Site: “Motoinari Kofun”
(Part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}) |map = Japan Kyoto city |map_dot_label = Motoinari Kofun |type = Zenpokoho-fun-South |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|WD=Q123117022}} }} '''Motoinari Kofun''' (元稲荷古墳) is an ancient Kofun located in {{ill|Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府向日市向日町北山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama, Mukocho, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja|向日丘陵古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Hills Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site within the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Key Kofuns in Mukō Hills{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/News/s-kouza/kouza242.pdf Minami Takao, "Terado Otsuka Kofun - Chief’s Tomb of the Early Kofun built in Mukō Hills"]}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Lecture, 2013).{{Sfn|4th Excavation Report Materials|2008}} !No.!!name!!type!!area |- |1||{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotsukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||91.2 m |- |2||'''Motoinari Kofun'''||{{ill|Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||94 m |- |3||[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Zenpo-koen-fun||95 m |- |4||{{ill|Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja|妙見山古墳 (向日市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myokenyama Kofun (Muko)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579270}}||Zenpo-koen-fun||114 m |- |5||{{ill|Densokaba Mound|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|1=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|lt=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Densokaba Mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}||65 m in diameter |}
The mound, built on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883263}} near the right bank of the [[:en:Katsura River|Katsura River]], is a large zenpokoho-fun (rectangular mounded tomb) dating back to the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century AD]]. Its name derives from the Inari shrine once located on the rear mound's summit. The site is part of a group of Kofuns in Mukō Hills, collectively known as the Mukō Hills Kofuns. == burial == The mound features a {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in its central rear section. Measuring 5.56 m in length and 1.9 m in height, the chamber demonstrates unique construction techniques and includes clay layers coated with vermilion. Despite historical looting, a few burial burial goods, such as iron weapons and pottery fragments, have been recovered. == National Historic Site == In 2016, Motoinari Kofun was included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofungun|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofungun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491395}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/shitei.html|title=National Historic Sites Database: Otokuni Kofungun|publisher=Historic Site Online}} == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kyoto University Museum|Kyoto University Museum]]: Houses and displays burial goods excavated from Motoinari Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Motoinari Kofun}} * [http://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755802446.html Official page for Motoinari Kofun (Mukō City)] * [http://www.muko-kankou.jp/map/detail.html?id=60 Mukō City Tourism Association: Motoinari Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|元稲荷古墳|version=100827937|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] {{Commons category|Motoinari Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279257}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Qq]]
